# Brite Project Overview

## What is Brite?

**brite** is a minimal CLI tool for personal content management based on Markdown. It allows users to create, convert, and publish content on their own stack without relying on external CMS services.

- **Repository**: `github.com/taka1156/brite`
- **Language**: Go
- **Module path**: `github.com/taka1156/brite`

---

## Key Concepts

| Term | Description |
|---|---|
| Article | A Markdown file with YAML front matter stored in `articleDir`. The filename (slug) is a UUID auto-generated at creation. |
| Front matter | YAML block at the top of each `.md` file containing `title`, `thumbnail`, `category`, `tags`, `created_at`, `updated_at`. |
| Config (`brite.json`) | Project-level configuration file that defines directories, whitelisted categories/tags, and optional R2 settings. |
| Cache (`.caches.json`) | Tracks image file sizes to diff and sync only changed images on publish. Stored in `cacheDir`. |
| Output JSON | Three JSON files (`all.json`, `category.json`, `tag.json`) generated from Markdown files and written to `outputDir`. |

---

## Directory Layout

```
<project root>/
├── brite.json          # Config file (generated by `brite init`)
├── <articleDir>/       # Markdown articles (e.g. articles/)
├── <imageDir>/         # Images organized by type (article/, category/, tag/)
├── <outputDir>/        # Generated JSON output (all.json, category.json, tag.json)
└── <cacheDir>/         # Image diff cache (.caches.json)
```

---

## Commands

| Command | Internal function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `brite init` | `InitializeConfigCommand.Initialize` | Generates a default `brite.json` in the current directory. Fails if one already exists. |
| `brite setup` | `SetupProjectCommand.Setup` | Creates the directory structure defined in `brite.json` (subdirectories under `articleDir` and `imageDir`). |
| `brite new` | `AddArticleCommand.Add` | Interactively creates a new Markdown article. Prompts for title (free text), category (single select), tags (multi select). Slug is auto-generated UUID; `created_at` is set to current time in RFC 3339. |
| `brite convert` | `ConvertArticleCommand.Convert` | Scans all `.md` files in `articleDir`, parses YAML front matter, and writes `all.json`, `category.json`, `tag.json` to `outputDir`. Articles with unregistered categories or tags are skipped with a warning. |
| `brite publish` | `PublishArticleCommand.Publish` | Diffs local images against `.caches.json`, syncs added/modified/deleted files to Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible), then re-uploads all output JSON files. |

All commands accept an optional `--config-path` flag (default: `brite.json`).

---

## Source Code Structure

```
cmd/brite/main.go                        # Entry point
internal/
  app/
    app.go                               # App struct, command dispatch (Run), DI wiring
    app_interface.go                     # Command interfaces (HelpCommand, AddCommand, etc.)
  command/
    add/add_article.go                   # `brite new` implementation
    convert/convert_article.go           # `brite convert` implementation
    publish/publish_article.go           # `brite publish` implementation
    initialize/initialize_config.go      # `brite init` implementation
    setup/setup_project.go               # `brite setup` implementation
    help/help_brite.go                   # `brite help` implementation
  infra/
    storage/storage.go                   # Storage interface (Upload / Delete)
    storage/r2.go                        # Cloudflare R2 implementation (NewS3Client, NewR2Storage)
  entity/
    config.go                            # Data types: BriteConfig, PostSummary, Post, ResponseData, ImageCache, etc.
    constants.go                         # Constants: output file names, cache file name, config file name, schema URL
  utils/
    utils.go                             # Shared helpers (LoadJson generic function)
```

---

## Data Models

### `BriteConfig` (maps to `brite.json`)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `$schema` | string | — | JSON Schema URL |
| `articleDir` | string | yes | Directory for `.md` files |
| `imageDir` | string | yes | Root directory for images |
| `outputDir` | string | yes | Output directory for JSON files |
| `cacheDir` | string | yes | Directory for `.caches.json` |
| `categories` | []string | yes | Whitelist of allowed categories |
| `tags` | []string | yes | Whitelist of allowed tags |
| `r2.bucketName` | string | no | R2 bucket name |
| `r2.baseUrl` | string | no | Base URL for image delivery |

### `PostSummary` (YAML front matter + slug)

| Field | YAML key | JSON key | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Slug` | — (derived from filename) | `slug` | UUID filename without extension |
| `Title` | `title` | `title` | Article title |
| `Thumbnail` | `thumbnail` | `thumbnail` | Thumbnail image path |
| `Category` | `category` | `category` | Single category (must be in whitelist) |
| `Tags` | `tags` | `tags` | Multiple tags (must be in whitelist) |
| `CreatedAt` | `created_at` | `created_at` | RFC 3339 creation timestamp |
| `UpdatedAt` | `updated_at` | `updated_at` | RFC 3339 last-updated timestamp |

### Output JSON shape

- **`all.json`**: `ResponseData` — contains `all` (array of full `Post`), `byCategory` (map of category→PostSummary[]), `byTag` (map of tag→PostSummary[]).
- **`category.json`**: `map[string][]PostSummary` — keyed by category name.
- **`tag.json`**: `map[string][]PostSummary` — keyed by tag name.

Within each category/tag list, articles are sorted by `created_at` descending.

---

## Publish & R2 Integration

- Uses `aws-sdk-go-v2` with a custom S3-compatible endpoint for Cloudflare R2.
- Image sync is **incremental**: only `Added`, `Modified`, or `Deleted` files are uploaded/deleted.
- Output JSON files are **fully re-uploaded** on every publish.
- All uploaded files are served with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`.
- `.caches.json` must be committed to version control for correct diff detection in CI.

### Required environment variables for `brite publish`

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | R2 access key ID |
| `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | R2 secret access key |
| `R2_ENDPOINT` | S3-compatible endpoint URL |
| `ENABLE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINTS` | `"true"` if path-style URLs are required |

---

## Build & Development

```bash
# Format
make fmt

# Run from source
make run RUN_ARGS="init"

# Build binary to ./sample/brite
make build

# Cross-compile for distribution
make dist
```

Supported distribution targets: `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`, `darwin/amd64`, `darwin/arm64`, `windows/amd64`.

---

## Key Design Decisions

- **UUID slugs**: Article filenames are UUID v4, preventing slug collisions and allowing headless-CMS-style referencing.
- **Whitelist-based taxonomy**: Only categories and tags declared in `brite.json` are accepted; unknown values cause the article to be skipped (not an error).
- **No database**: All state is file-based — Markdown files, `brite.json`, and `.caches.json`.
- **S3-compatible abstraction**: The publish command targets any S3-compatible storage; Cloudflare R2 is the recommended backend due to its free egress tier.
